name: Ape
ecology_notes: Apes dwell in warm forests and plains, living in social troops. They are territorial and will defend their range aggressively.

name: Ape, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire apes are larger, more aggressive cousins of common apes, found in the deepest warm forests. They are solitary or form small family groups.

name: Badger
ecology_notes: Badgers are burrowing animals found in temperate woodlands and plains. They are notorious for their ferocity when cornered, entering a frenzied rage.

name: Badger, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire badgers are hulking, immensely tough predators that burrow through the earth with ease. Their rage when wounded makes them extremely dangerous prey.

name: Bat Swarm
ecology_notes: Bat swarms roost in caves, hollow trees, and ruins. When disturbed they pour forth as a living storm of tiny biting mammals, disorienting and wounding anything nearby.

name: Bear, Black
ecology_notes: Black bears inhabit forested regions in both temperate and cold climates. Omnivorous and generally shy, they become dangerous when threatened or defending cubs.

name: Bear, Brown
ecology_notes: Brown (grizzly) bears are apex predators of temperate and cold forests. Their ability to grapple prey makes them formidable hunters.

name: Bear, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire bears are prehistoric behemoths that lurk in ancient forests. Solitary and territorial, they will pursue prey for miles and are nearly impossible to drive off.

name: Bear, Polar
ecology_notes: Polar bears are expert swimmers adapted to arctic seas. They hunt seals on ice floes and fish in frigid waters, their thick white fur providing camouflage in the snow.

name: Bison
ecology_notes: Bison roam vast plains in large herds. When frightened, a stampede can be catastrophic. They are a keystone species of the grassland ecosystem.

name: Boar
ecology_notes: Boars are aggressive wild pigs known for their ferocity; they continue fighting even below 0 hp. They root through forest floors for tubers, grubs, and carrion.

name: Boar, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire boars are massive prehistoric pigs that crash through the undergrowth. Their ferocity makes them feared even by experienced hunters.

name: Camel
ecology_notes: Camels are desert-adapted pack animals capable of traveling great distances without water. They are notoriously ill-tempered and will bite or kick without provocation.

name: Cat
ecology_notes: Cats are small, nimble hunters found near settlements and in wild forests alike. They are prized as mousers and companions.

name: Cheetah
ecology_notes: Cheetahs are the fastest land animals, capable of explosive sprints. They rely on speed and a tripping tackle to bring down prey on open plains.

name: Crocodile
ecology_notes: Crocodiles lurk in tropical waterways as ambush predators. They can hold their breath for long periods, surfacing silently to drag prey underwater.

name: Crocodile, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire crocodiles are titanic reptiles that can capsize small boats and swallow humanoids whole. They lurk in the deepest river channels and coastal swamps.

name: Dog
ecology_notes: Dogs are domesticated canines found near civilization. They are loyal companions and useful working animals, prized for their tracking ability and alertness.

name: Dog, Riding
ecology_notes: Riding dogs are large, powerful breeds trained to carry small riders such as halflings or gnomes. They make excellent war mounts and guards.

name: Donkey
ecology_notes: Donkeys are hardy pack animals prized for their sure-footedness on rough terrain and their resistance to panic. They are stubbornly resistant to commands they find unreasonable.

name: Eagle
ecology_notes: Eagles are keen-eyed raptors that hunt from great heights. Their eyesight is legendary, allowing them to spot small prey from hundreds of feet in the air.

name: Eagle, Giant
ecology_notes: Giant eagles are intelligent, noble creatures that nest on the highest mountain peaks. They sometimes form alliances with good-aligned beings and serve as mounts or guardians.

name: Elephant
ecology_notes: Elephants are the largest land animals, living in matriarchal herds on warm plains and forests. They are highly intelligent for animals and possess long memories.

name: Frog, Giant
ecology_notes: Giant frogs lurk at the water's edge in swamps, striking prey with a long sticky tongue before swallowing them whole. They can leap extraordinary distances.

name: Hawk
ecology_notes: Hawks are swift aerial hunters that dive on small prey from high altitude. Their exceptional Spot bonus makes them popular as familiars.

name: Horse, Heavy
ecology_notes: Heavy horses are powerful draft animals used for labor and transport. They are not trained for combat but will kick if cornered.

name: Horse, Light
ecology_notes: Light horses are swift, agile riding animals suited for long-distance travel. They are the most common mount for cavalry and adventurers.

name: Horse, Warhorse (Heavy)
ecology_notes: Heavy warhorses are specially trained for battlefield combat. They are conditioned not to panic and will attack enemies on command, making them prized cavalry mounts.

name: Horse, Warhorse (Light)
ecology_notes: Light warhorses are fast, combat-trained mounts used by cavalry. Their speed and training make them excellent for skirmishing and pursuit.

name: Hyena
ecology_notes: Hyenas are pack hunters and scavengers of warm savannahs and deserts. Their bone-crushing jaws and trip attack make them effective pack predators.

name: Leopard
ecology_notes: Leopards are solitary ambush hunters that stalk prey from trees or rocky outcroppings. Their pounce-and-rake combination makes them devastatingly effective against single targets.

name: Lion
ecology_notes: Lions are apex predators of warm grasslands, living in social prides. Their pounce and rake combination overwhelms prey in seconds.

name: Lion, Dire
ecology_notes: Dire lions are massive prehistoric felines that dominate entire regions of warm savannah. They are solitary apex predators capable of bringing down creatures much larger than themselves.

name: Mammoth
ecology_notes: Mammoths are massive, shaggy elephants adapted to cold climates. Their enormous curved tusks are used to dig through snow for forage and as devastating weapons.

name: Manta Ray
ecology_notes: Manta rays are large, graceful filter-feeders of warm and temperate seas. They are generally passive but will use their tail sting defensively if harassed.

name: Monkey
ecology_notes: Monkeys are agile, social primates of warm forests. They travel in troops through the canopy, communicating with calls and occasionally raiding crops.

name: Mule
ecology_notes: Mules are the hardy offspring of horses and donkeys, prized as pack animals for their sure-footedness and extraordinary endurance. They are notably resistant to panic.
